steps.title: Identify the reporting structure needed
steps.content:
The sentence must report what Losey has argued about when domestication began.

After the introductory dependent clause (about the discovery), the main clause should directly express the researcher's claim using standard reported speech convention: a verb of saying or arguing plus a “that” clause to introduce what was claimed.

steps.title: Apply the reported speech convention
steps.content:
Option A uses “researcher Robert Losey has argued that domestication may have begun much earlier” — this follows the standard English pattern for reporting claims, with the verb “has argued” directly stating the researcher's action, followed by “that” introducing the content of his argument.

This makes the attribution to Losey clear and direct. Options B and D nominalize the idea as “argument is that,” which adds unnecessary words and distance between the researcher and his claim.

Option C avoids “that” and places Losey's argument as a parenthetical interruption, which doesn't follow the conventional structure for reporting someone's claim.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
